Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to output a DuckDB SQL query that accomplishes steps. Use K as a parameter. Provide explanation? The user likely expects the query.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Provide a query that creates CTEs: ordered activities per case using LISTAGG with ordering, then count per variant, then rank, then filter top K, then join back to original table on case_id where variant in top K.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: DuckDB supports LISTAGG with ORDER BY inside. Eg: SELECT case_id, LISTAGG(activity, ', ') ORDER BY timestamp AS variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then count per variant: SELECT variant, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM ... GROUP BY variant

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Then top K: Use ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT K (use subquery). Then final: select e.* from event_log e join (select case_id from ... where variant in topK) t on e.case_id = t.case_id

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Let K be a parameter; we can use a placeholder like :K or use a value.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Ill produce query: WITH case_variant AS ( SELECT case_id, LISTAGG(activity, ', ') ORDER BY timestamp AS variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id ), variant_counts AS ( SELECT variant, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM case_variant GROUP BY variant ), top_variants AS ( SELECT variant FROM variant_counts ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT :K   -- replace :K with desired K ) SELECT e.* FROM event_log e JOIN case_variant cv ON e.case_id = cv.case_id WHERE cv.variant IN (SELECT variant FROM top_variants);

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Make sure we use correct LISTAGG syntax: DuckDBs LISTAGG(expr, separator) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ...) but also can be LISTAGG(activity, ', ') ORDER BY timestamp. Yes. Thus final answer.
